notes.total: 39 tickets scoped across 4 sprints — a manageable load that allowed the team to deliver 100% completion without scope creep.
notes.completed: All 39 tickets closed, achieving full delivery of quarter goals.
notes.completion_rate: 100% completion rate demonstrates excellent sprint discipline and effective planning.
notes.releases_shipped: 3 releases shipped across the quarter, maintaining a steady delivery cadence with a median of 1 release per sprint.
notes.oos_total: Zero out-of-sprint additions — sprint planning was disciplined and scope remained stable throughout the quarter.
notes.oos_open: No outstanding OOS items — clean slate with no unplanned debt carried forward.
notes.type_split: Zero bugs logged (34 stories, 5 tasks) — either exceptional quality or bugs are being tracked elsewhere, worth verifying.
notes.avg_releases: Median of 1 release per sprint indicates consistent shipping cadence, with 0.8 mean reflecting one sprint without a release.
notes.time_logged: Only 43 hours logged against 976 hours estimated (4% accuracy) — severe time tracking gap makes workload and capacity data unreliable.
notes.estimate_accuracy: 96% variance between estimated and logged time signals broken time tracking, not estimation issues — only 4% of work was logged.
notes.no_estimate: All tickets had estimates, but with only 43 of 976 hours logged, estimation discipline cannot be validated due to tracking gaps.
notes.rollover: Zero rollover — no tickets carried between sprints, indicating strong sprint commitment and predictable delivery.
notes.cycle_time: Median 18 days to complete tickets is elevated for a sprint-based workflow — investigate if blockers or handoffs are delaying closure.
notes.assignee_workload: Work reasonably distributed: Giuseppe (46%), Fabrizio (38%), Gianni (13%), Nikola (3%) — but time logging gaps make capacity assessment incomplete.
